properties.name: The High Line
properties.description: The High Line is a 1.45-mile-long elevated linear park, greenway, and rail trail created on a former New York Central Railroad spur on the west side of Manhattan in New York City.

properties.name: Manhattan Bridge
properties.description: The Manhattan Bridge is a suspension bridge that crosses the East River in New York City, connecting Lower Manhattan at Canal Street with Downtown Brooklyn. With a length of 6,855 ft it was designed by Leon Moisseiff.

properties.name: Rockefeller Center
properties.name:ja: ロックフェラー・センター

properties.name: Statue of Liberty
properties.description: The Statue of Liberty is a colossal neoclassical sculpture on Liberty Island in New York Harbor in New York City. The copper statue, a gift from the people of France, was designed by French sculptor Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi.
properties.inscription: Give me your tired, your poor, Your huddled masses yearning to breathe free, The wretched refuse of your teeming shore. Send these, the homeless, tempest-tost, to me, I lift my lamp beside the golden door !

properties.inscription: To commemorate the one hundredth anniversary of the inauguration of George Washington as first president of the United States

properties.name: Brooklyn Bridge
properties.description: The Brooklyn Bridge is a hybrid cable-stayed/suspension bridge in New York City, spanning the East River between the boroughs of Manhattan and Brooklyn. Opened on May 24, 1883, the Brooklyn Bridge was the first fixed crossing of the East River.

properties.description: Established in 1994, the Downtown Boathouse is an all-volunteer nonprofit organization dedicated to providing free public access to the harbor in New York City through our kayaking programs.
